etymology.description: semantic 一(一) (yī, "one, signifying a number") + phonetic 人(人) (rén) -> borrowing pronunciation to represent a large number
etymology.interpretation: The character is formed by adding a line to 人(人) (rén, "person"), signifying an extension, combined with 一(一) (yī, "one") to form the concept of a thousand – a milestone number reached through cumulative counting.

examples.chinese: 这本书要卖一千块钱(這本書要賣一千塊錢)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū yào mài yī qiān kuài qián.
examples.english: This book is selling for a thousand yuan.

examples.chinese: 长城每年吸引成千上万的游客(長城每年吸引成千上萬的遊客)。
examples.pinyin: Chángchéng měinián xīyǐn chéngqiānshàngwàn de yóukè.
examples.english: The Great Wall attracts thousands upon thousands of tourists every year.

examples.chinese: 她拥有数千本书(她擁有數千本書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒngyǒu shù qiān běn shū.
